title: Fixing Middleware Order for a Secure Express API

statement.markdown: The code below is attempting to implement a secure Express API with JWT-based authentication. However, it contains a subtle bug in the middleware order. The API should require authentication for all routes. Identify the bug and correct the middleware order.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a JSON object containing user credentials and request data.
statement.outputFormat: The API should return a JSON response with a success or error message.
statement.notes: The API should handle authentication and authorization for all routes.

constraints.description: The maximum number of users in the system

hints.text: The bug is related to the middleware order. Think about the correct order for authentication and route handling.

editorial.title: Editorial: Fixing Middleware Order for a Secure Express API
editorial.intuition: The correct middleware order should be: authentication, then route handling. The authentication middleware should be placed before the route handlers.
editorial.bruteForce: You can start by printing out the middleware order and checking the results for the first example input.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to move the authentication middleware before the route handlers, ensuring that all routes require authentication.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness involves showing that the authentication middleware is called before the route handlers for all routes.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: 1) Call the authentication middleware, 2) Call the route handlers, 3) Return the result.
commonMistakes: Not placing the authentication middleware before the route handlers
Not handling authentication for all routes
